mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals accelerating labor market deterioration, which immediately suppresses consumer spending expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or directional accuracy falls below 40%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real sales of existing homes and housing starts indicate a synchronized collapse in residential demand, which transmits to broader equity valuations through financial sector leverage and construction supply chains, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 60-day returns, with out-of-sample R-squared below 0.01 over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production reflects weakening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which reduces corporate profit margins and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days during credit-constrained regimes.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines do not predict negative 20-day returns when credit spreads exceed 100 basis points, or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses below 2.0%, real borrowing costs turn negative, stimulating equity risk premiums and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive real rate differentials consistently coincide with negative 20-day SPX returns, yielding directional accuracy below 40%.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect sustained labor market expansion, which supports aggregate consumption and corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero predictive power for 60-day equity returns, with Rank IC below 0.01 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A quarter-over-quarter decline in real gross domestic product confirms a contraction in aggregate demand, which immediately triggers risk-off positioning and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days during recessionary contractions.
failure_condition: GDP contractions fail to predict negative 5-day returns when market volatility indices remain below 15, or directional accuracy < 45%.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where industrial production declines while total payrolls remain flat or rise signals rising labor costs and falling productivity, which compresses profit margins and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The INDPRO-PAYEMS divergence metric yields a negative Rank IC over a 3-year rolling window, or directional accuracy falls below 45%.
